romania.title: FABER prezintă expoziția „Legături Chimice” curatoriată de Martina Muzi la prima ediție a București Design Festival
romania.summary: FABER aduce în capitală expoziția „Legături Chimice”, un proiect multidisciplinar care explorează intersecția dintre design, industrie și cercetare. Vernisajul va avea loc miercuri, 27 mai 2026, începând cu ora 18:00,… Articolul FABER prezintă expoziția „Legături Chimice” curatoriată de Martina Muzi la prima ediție a București Design Festival apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.title: HazarDance 2026 – caleidoscop al dansului contemporan. Festivalul are loc între 16 – 21 mai 2026, la Iași
romania.summary: Între 16 și 21 mai 2026, la Iași are loc cea de-a cincea ediție a HazarDance Festival, manifestare ce reunește talente din lumea dansului, atât naționale, cât și internaționale, transformând… Articolul HazarDance 2026 – caleidoscop al dansului contemporan. Festivalul are loc între 16 – 21 mai 2026, la Iași apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.title: Corul Madrigal transformă Piața Matache în scenă, de Ziua Bujorului Românesc
romania.summary: Corul Madrigal, dirijat de Anna Ungureanu, susține recitalul extraordinar dedicat bujorului românesc, în cadrul zilelor „Bujori în Matache”. Evenimentul are loc sâmbătă, 16 mai 2026, de la ora 19:00, la… Articolul Corul Madrigal transformă Piața Matache în scenă, de Ziua Bujorului Românesc apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.title: Viitorul meseriei de profesor
romania.summary: Am stat de vorbă cu Letiția Suciu, Director Executiv – Asociația Școala Încrederii, despre atractivitatea meseriei de profesor, provocările la început de carieră didactică, noua paradigmă a educației și despre… Articolul Viitorul meseriei de profesor apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.title: Asociația Tășuleasa Social inaugurează primul „Modul de găzduire pe Via  Transilvanica” la Șapartoc, în județul Mureș
romania.summary: Asociația Tășuleasa Social continuă dezvoltarea infrastructurii celui mai mare traseu de lungă distanță din România prin inaugurarea oficială a primului modul de găzduire Via Transilvanica. Situat în satul Șapartoc din… Articolul Asociația Tășuleasa Social inaugurează primul „Modul de găzduire pe Via Transilvanica” la Șapartoc, în județul Mureș apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.title: APCAO lansează primul ghid de recuperare pentru copiii cu afecțiuni hematologice maligne, cu sprijinul Kaizen Foundation
romania.summary: APCAO (Asociația Părinților Copiilor cu Afecțiuni Oncologice) anunță lansarea oficială a ghidului multidisciplinar „Recuperarea Medicală a Copiilor cu Afecțiuni Hematologice Maligne”, o resursă de referință pentru îngrijirea oncologică pediatrică din România, dezvoltată cu sprijinul Kaizen Foundation.

romania.title: Subiecte Evaluarea Națională 2026, clasa a II-a: Elevii au susținut proba la Matematică și Explorarea mediului. Când se anunță notele
romania.summary: Elevii de clasa a II-a au susținut astăzi, 14 mai, proba scrisă la Matematică și Explorarea mediului, în cadrul Evaluării Naționale 2026, după ce marți au avut testarea scrisă la Limba română, iar miercuri, 13 mai, au fost evaluați la competențele de citire.

romania.title: 😂 Oficialii care au stat până târziu la Summitul B9, invitaţi să se dreagă la Summitul de Potroace
romania.summary: Orice s-ar zice, România ştie să organizeze un summit de succes! Potrivit mai multor surse oficiale, liderii ţărilor participante la reuniunea B9 s-au distrat de minune aseară, până pe la 4 dimineaţa, când au semnat Declaraţia comună „Ş-altă dată, ş-altă dată o s-o facem şi mai, şi mai lată” şi s-au retras în camerele lor. romania.title: 😂 Un băiețel de 5 ani a stat 2 nopți în pădure, timp în care mii de bucureșteni s-au plâns că n-au căldură
romania.summary: Un studiu antropologic a scos la iveală că în cele două zile în care un băiețel de 5 ani dintr-un sat sibian a stat singur în pădure, 174.000 de bucureșteni s-au plâns pe social media că n-au destulă căldură sau apă caldă. „În locul acelor smiorcăiți nu aș mai ieși din casă o lună”, a […] The post Un băiețel de 5 ani a stat 2 nopți în pădure, timp în care mii de bucureșteni s-au plâns că n-au căldură appeared first on Times New Roman .

Vizita lui MIhai Neamțu în Uganda, unde a asistat la ceremonia preluării unui nou mandat de către dictatorul țării, a fost subiect de glume și desconsiderare pentru deputatul român. Dar iată că în apărarea lui Mihai Neamțu a sărit chiar dictatorul […] The post Mihai Neamțu l-a impresionat profund pe dictatorul din Uganda: „Are buze foarte moi!” appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.summary: Programul național Telefonul Vârstnicului – 0800 460 001 Sprijinul media a venit de la Spot TV – Pro TV, TVR, Antena 1, Antena 3 CNN, Antena Stars, Prima TV, Prima News, Pro Cinema, Pro Arena, Acasă, Acasă Gold, Trinitas TV, Speranța TV, Antena 3 Deva,... romania.summary: Cine suntem noi și de cine avem nevoie? Suntem echipa de la Telefonul Vârstnicului, serviciu social licențiat, singura linie telefonică gratuită și confidențială, accesibilă la nivel național, dedicată persoanelor vârstnice, ce oferă consiliere socială și alinarea singurătății. În fiecare an suntem sunați de peste 5,000... Post-ul Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București! apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

romania.summary: București, aprilie 2026 – Într-o lume în care cererea de hrană crește accelerat, iar presiunea asupra resurselor naturale este tot... Articolul Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.summary: Într-o casă modestă din județul Vaslui, unde mirosul de lemne arse se amestecă cu zgomotul pașilor mici de copii și... Articolul „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase. apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.title: De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță
romania.summary: În timp ce pentru mulți români Paștele este sărbătoarea revederii și a meselor în familie, pentru mulți vârstnici singuri aceste zile sunt printre cele mai grele din an. Singurătatea doare, lipsurile și sentimentul de abandon se simt mai apăsat tocmai atunci când toți ceilalți par... Post-ul De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

medical.title: Common NSAIDs in first trimester show no birth defect link, data suggest
medical.summary: Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), such as ibuprofen and naproxen, taken during the first trimester of pregnancy are not associated with an increased risk of major birth defects, according to a new study published in PLOS Medicine by Sharon Daniel of Ben-Gurion University of the Negev and Clalit Health Services, Beer-Sheva, Israel, and colleagues.

medical.title: Depressed mice successfully treated with smart contact lenses that zap their brains: New study
medical.summary: Scientists in South Korea have developed experimental contact lenses designed to send electrical signals through the retina and into brain regions linked to mood. In mice, the technology appeared to improve depression-like behavior.

medical.title: France and Netherlands say all hantavirus contact cases negative
medical.summary: France's health minister said Thursday that 26 people in France identified as close contacts of hantavirus cases linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship had tested negative for the illness.

medical.title: The rules neurons follow to make sense of what we see
medical.summary: Even in the primary visual cortex, a brain region named for its specialized role in processing basic features of what the eyes see, not every neuron ends up answering the call to process properties of visual input. Maybe that's because each neuron receives a wide variety of inputs via thousands of circuit connections, or "synapses," and has to opt to respond to the visual information vs. something else.

medical.title: Steroid use falls, but creatine use climbs rapidly, study shows
medical.summary: U.S. teens report far less anabolic steroid use than they did two decades ago, but creatine use has risen rapidly in recent years, according to a new University of Michigan study. Combined with declining perceptions of steroid harm and slightly softer disapproval of steroid use, the findings suggest teens may be growing more accepting of muscle-enhancing products, including legal supplements and potentially steroids.

medical.title: Experimental drug shrinks advanced kidney tumors, clears cancer in one patient
medical.summary: A new Phase I clinical trial provides proof of concept for a potential therapy for treatment-resistant cancers, particularly kidney cancer. In the new study, published in Cell Reports Medicine, researchers tested a novel kind of immunotherapy in individuals with different types of cancer.

medical.title: Dinner at the door: Convenient healthy meals may ease depression symptoms
medical.summary: Making healthy meals more convenient through meal delivery services could improve depressive symptoms by removing some of the daily burdens that often accompany depression, according to a new University of Michigan study.

medical.title: Study finds one in eight adults carries hidden genetic risk—and reveals what it takes to act on it
medical.summary: When Mayo Clinic researchers sequenced the genomes of 484 seemingly healthy adults, they found that about 13% carried a serious, previously unrecognized genetic risk—conditions those patients did not know about and that standard care would likely miss.

medical.title: Sleep and diet may matter more than exercise for buffering the health toll of chronic stress
medical.summary: When work gets stressful, the standard advice is familiar: exercise more, eat better, sleep more, and cut back on unhealthy habits. But our new research study suggests not all healthy habits offer the same protection from chronic work stress.

medical.title: How understanding art history can help doctors treat obesity
medical.summary: Understanding how obesity has been portrayed in art across the ages can make doctors more empathetic and less judgmental, which should lead to their patients achieving better outcomes, new research at the European Congress on Obesity (ECO 2026) argues.

medical.title: The liver's immune cells might be the key to curing hepatitis B
medical.summary: Fifteen years ago, doctors in Europe noticed a remarkable thing happening in people with chronic hepatitis B infections. When patients went off their medications, the virus started to come back—and then some of the patients were cured. Something was goading their immune systems to go after the virus as the infection rebounded.

medical.title: This simple amino acid supplement greatly reduces Alzheimer’s damage
medical.summary: A new study suggests a surprisingly simple compound could help fight Alzheimer’s disease. Researchers found that arginine—an inexpensive amino acid already considered safe—can reduce the buildup of toxic amyloid proteins in the brain, a hallmark of the disease. In animal models, oral arginine not only lowered harmful protein deposits but also improved behavior and reduced brain inflammation.

science.title: Webb discovers one of the universe's first galaxies
science.summary: Scientists have discovered a galaxy as it was 13 billion years ago, 800 million years after the Big Bang. It contains possible evidence of the universe's first stars and is one of the most chemically primitive galaxies observed to date.

science.title: A new method could help Washington shellfish farmers control a pesky shrimp
science.summary: Burrowing shrimp are small marine excavators native to Washington. They make their homes deep in the sediment by digging, turning the ground to Swiss cheese. This presents a problem for shellfish farmers, whose clams and oysters are often smothered under layers of displaced sediment.

science.title: Summers are getting longer each year, and it isn't all fun and games
science.summary: Do you have the sense that summers feel different than when you were younger? That they start earlier, arrive quickly and remain intense until the fall? If you live in the mid-latitudes of either the Northern or Southern Hemispheres, chances are you answered yes.

science.title: New AI system classifies India rainfall better, cutting false alarms and missed heavy rain
science.summary: AI can predict rainfall intensity better than several widely used forecasting models in tests using historical weather data from India. The new model reported in the International Journal of Mobile Communications shows that combining different forms of AI, along with advanced data-cleaning and optimization techniques, can make rainfall prediction more accurate and reliable, particularly when expressed in practical categories such as light, moderate, or heavy rain.

science.title: NASA's Psyche spacecraft buzzing Mars on its way to a rare metal asteroid
science.summary: A NASA spacecraft chasing a rare metal asteroid swings past Mars this week for a gravity boost, snapping thousands of pictures as practice for the main encounter in 2029.

science.title: Combinations of climate extremes may prompt carbon budget rethink
science.summary: Combined extreme climate events are likely to become more common in the future if carbon emissions continue to rise, a paper in Nature suggests. The study finds that the frequency of compound events—such as concurrent hot–wet and drought–heat extremes—is linked to cumulative carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions. In particular, the frequency of more severe events is predicted to escalate rapidly.

science.title: Discovery of antimicrobial peptides in ant venom has far-reaching implications
science.summary: In addition to serving as biochemical weapons for offense and defense, the venoms produced by ants in the subfamily Formicinae also fulfill additional roles. For example, the ants use it to protect their nests from pathogens. It has long been assumed that the primary constituent of these venoms, formic acid, was responsible for these functions.

science.title: Large-scale eDNA survey reveals hidden factors that affect regional fish communities
science.summary: As climate change and human activities continually ramp up, fish are forced to find ways to adapt. As fish move around to find more suitable habitats as ocean conditions shift, regional fish distributions change—which can have huge downstream effects on local ecosystems.

science.title: Mathematicians prove existence of Kaleidocycles then unlock their exact motion
science.summary: Kaleidocycles are flexible polyhedral structures composed of rigid tetrahedra connected along their edges to form rotating rings. Each tetrahedron is a solid 3D polygon with four triangular faces (like a triangular pyramid), and the hinges connect neighboring units, enabling a smooth rotational motion of the ring without deforming the individual pieces. These mechanisms are often compared with the bubble rings blown by dolphins.

science.title: NASA, Industry Prepare Cryogenic Fuel Technology Demo
science.summary: NASA is collaborating with Eta Space of Rockledge, Florida, on an in‑orbit technology demonstration to advance a key capability for future deep space missions. The Liquid Oxygen Flight Demonstration, or LOXSAT, will test cryogenic fluid management technologies necessary for creating in-space propellant depots, essentially gas stations in space, that could support long-term exploration. During a […]

science.title: Astrophysicists use 'space archaeology' to trace the history of a spiral galaxy
science.summary: Billions of years ago, a young spiral galaxy began to grow in a crowded part of the universe. It pulled in gas and small companion galaxies, slowly building up the bright central region and sweeping spiral arms we see today.

science.title: Will future missions to the moon be sustainable? It may depend on whom you ask
science.summary: There's a new space race to the moon, and this time the ambitions are not just to visit but to stay. NASA's Artemis program aims to establish a long-term human presence on the lunar surface in the 2030s. China, India, Japan and a number of private companies all have lunar mission programs of their own.

science.title: String theory is uniquely derived from basic assumptions about the universe, physicists show
science.summary: If you could take an apple and break it into smaller and smaller parts, you would find molecules, then atoms, followed by subatomic particles like protons and the quarks and gluons that make them up. You might think you hit the bottom, but, according to string theorists, if you keep going to even smaller scales—about a billion billion times smaller than a proton—you will find more: tiny vibrating strings.

science.title: Sex-related differences in hoverfly eyes give insight into their aerodynamic powers
science.summary: Many male hoverflies have bigger eyes than females, giving them the advantage of better optics and faster photoreceptors in high-speed pursuits to find a preferred partner to breed.

science.title: A child's environment may shape how their brain solves problems
science.summary: For decades, researchers have documented an achievement gap between children from higher- and lower-income families. On average, children with more resources perform better in school and on cognitive tests.

science.title: Study finds more than 84% of dogs show signs of fear, anxiety
science.summary: A dog trembling during a thunderstorm or backing away from a stranger may seem like an isolated reaction—yet new research suggests these moments are far from rare. In fact, the majority of dogs may experience some level of fear or anxiety more often than their owners, according to a new study that analyzed behavior data from tens of thousands of pets across the United States.

environment.title: This 2-pound dinosaur is rewriting what scientists know about evolution
environment.summary: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dinosaurs. The fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis reveals that these animals became tiny before developing their later specialized features, such as stubby arms and ant-eating adaptations. Weighing under two pounds, the dinosaur is one of the smallest known from South America.

environment.title: Scientists finally solve the mystery of yeast’s tiny centromeres
environment.summary: Scientists have uncovered how brewer’s yeast developed its unusually tiny centromeres, the DNA regions that guide chromosome separation during cell division. By studying related yeast species, researchers found centromeres that appear to represent evolutionary halfway points. These structures seem to have formed from retrotransposons—mobile “jumping genes” in the genome. The discovery shows how DNA once considered genomic junk can be transformed into essential chromosome machinery.

environment.title: Light-guided evolution creates proteins that can switch, sense, and compute
environment.summary: Researchers have created a method called optovolution that uses light to guide the evolution of proteins with dynamic behaviors. By engineering yeast cells so their survival depended on proteins switching states at the right time, scientists could rapidly select the best-performing variants. The technique produced new light-sensitive proteins that respond to different colors and improved optogenetic systems. It even evolved a protein that behaves like a tiny logic gate, activating genes only when two signals are present.

environment.title: Scientists discover hidden species among Borneo’s “fanged frogs”
environment.summary: DNA is revealing that many animals once thought to be a single species may actually be several hidden ones. But research on Bornean fanged frogs shows the line between species can be blurry—an important challenge when deciding what wildlife needs protection most.

environment.title: Lake Coatepeque
environment.summary: Set amid El Salvador’s modern, active volcanic landscape, tranquil blue waters fill a caldera formed by ancient eruptions. The post Lake Coatepeque appeared first on NASA Science .

environment.title: Scientists stunned to find signs of ancient life in a place no one expected
environment.summary: While exploring ancient seabeds in Morocco, scientists discovered strange wrinkle-like textures in deep-water sediments that shouldn’t have been there. These structures are usually made by sunlight-loving microbial mats in shallow waters. But the rocks formed far below the reach of light, suggesting a different explanation. Evidence points to chemosynthetic microbes—organisms powered by chemical reactions—creating the mats in the dark depths of an ancient ocean.

environment.title: Scientists finally reveal why mint feels cold
environment.summary: Scientists have revealed how the body’s microscopic cold sensor, TRPM8, detects both chilly temperatures and the cooling effect of menthol. The discovery finally shows how the sensation of “cool” works at the molecular level—and could inspire new treatments for pain and eye disorders.

environment.title: Bird droppings helped build one of ancient Peru’s most powerful kingdoms
environment.summary: New research suggests seabird guano helped transform the Chincha Kingdom into one of the most prosperous societies in ancient Peru. Chemical clues in centuries-old maize show farmers fertilized their crops with guano gathered from nearby islands, dramatically boosting yields in the desert landscape. The resulting agricultural surplus fueled trade, population growth, and regional influence.

mix_items.summary: Tiana are 21 de ani și face voluntariat de aproximativ 3 ani în programele și activitățile World Vision România. A crescut... Articolul Când dorința de a face bine este mai puternică decât emoțiile: Tiana și dragostea ei pentru voluntariat apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.summary: Locație: București Fii adultul care poate schimba viitorul unui copil Dacă ai răbdare, empatie și îți dorești un job cu adevărat valoros, în care fiecare zi contează pentru dezvoltarea unor... The post Psihopedagog/Pedagog appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

mix_items.title: 😂 Un băiețel de 5 ani a stat 2 nopți în pădure, timp în care mii de bucureșteni s-au plâns că n-au căldură
mix_items.summary: Un studiu antropologic a scos la iveală că în cele două zile în care un băiețel de 5 ani dintr-un sat sibian a stat singur în pădure, 174.000 de bucureșteni s-au plâns pe social media că n-au destulă căldură sau apă caldă. „În locul acelor smiorcăiți nu aș mai ieși din casă o lună”, a […] The post Un băiețel de 5 ani a stat 2 nopți în pădure, timp în care mii de bucureșteni s-au plâns că n-au căldură appeared first on Times New Roman .

mix_items.title: APCAO lansează primul ghid de recuperare pentru copiii cu afecțiuni hematologice maligne, cu sprijinul Kaizen Foundation
mix_items.summary: APCAO (Asociația Părinților Copiilor cu Afecțiuni Oncologice) anunță lansarea oficială a ghidului multidisciplinar „Recuperarea Medicală a Copiilor cu Afecțiuni Hematologice Maligne”, o resursă de referință pentru îngrijirea oncologică pediatrică din România, dezvoltată cu sprijinul Kaizen Foundation.

mix_items.title: Subiecte Evaluarea Națională 2026, clasa a II-a: Elevii au susținut proba la Matematică și Explorarea mediului. Când se anunță notele
mix_items.summary: Elevii de clasa a II-a au susținut astăzi, 14 mai, proba scrisă la Matematică și Explorarea mediului, în cadrul Evaluării Naționale 2026, după ce marți au avut testarea scrisă la Limba română, iar miercuri, 13 mai, au fost evaluați la competențele de citire.

mix_items.title: Schimbările mari încep cu pași mici: povestea lui Robert și drumul lui către advocacy la nivel internațional
mix_items.summary: Robert are 18 ani și este reprezentant al copiilor în Consiliul Consultativ al Copiilor și Tinerilor din cadrul World Vision România. Prin... Articolul Schimbările mari încep cu pași mici: povestea lui Robert și drumul lui către advocacy la nivel internațional apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.summary: Alexandru are 10 ani, locuiește într-un sat din județul Giurgiu și este elev în clasa a III‑a. Este un copil cu... Articolul Visul lui Alexandru începe cu o minge apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.summary: Programul național Telefonul Vârstnicului – 0800 460 001 Sprijinul media a venit de la Spot TV – Pro TV, TVR, Antena 1, Antena 3 CNN, Antena Stars, Prima TV, Prima News, Pro Cinema, Pro Arena, Acasă, Acasă Gold, Trinitas TV, Speranța TV, Antena 3 Deva,... Post-ul SPRIJIN MEDIA OFERIT PROGRAMELOR FUNDAȚIEI REGALE ÎN ANUL 2025 apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

mix_items.title: Webb discovers one of the universe's first galaxies
mix_items.summary: Scientists have discovered a galaxy as it was 13 billion years ago, 800 million years after the Big Bang. It contains possible evidence of the universe's first stars and is one of the most chemically primitive galaxies observed to date.

mix_items.title: Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București!
mix_items.summary: Cine suntem noi și de cine avem nevoie? Suntem echipa de la Telefonul Vârstnicului, serviciu social licențiat, singura linie telefonică gratuită și confidențială, accesibilă la nivel național, dedicată persoanelor vârstnice, ce oferă consiliere socială și alinarea singurătății. În fiecare an suntem sunați de peste 5,000... Post-ul Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București! apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

mix_items.title: Simona, eleva care descoperă viitorul industriei alimentare pas cu pas
mix_items.summary: Un drum construit prin educație Simona este elevă în clasa a XI-a, la specializarea Tehnician în industria alimentară, la un... Articolul Simona, eleva care descoperă viitorul industriei alimentare pas cu pas apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: A new method could help Washington shellfish farmers control a pesky shrimp
mix_items.summary: Burrowing shrimp are small marine excavators native to Washington. They make their homes deep in the sediment by digging, turning the ground to Swiss cheese. This presents a problem for shellfish farmers, whose clams and oysters are often smothered under layers of displaced sediment.

mix_items.title: Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale
mix_items.summary: București, aprilie 2026 – Într-o lume în care cererea de hrană crește accelerat, iar presiunea asupra resurselor naturale este tot... Articolul Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Psiholog
mix_items.summary: Locație: București | Nivel carieră: Experiență de minimum 1 an Candidatul Ideal: Ești tu vocea de care au nevoie? Un copil are nevoie de iubire, siguranță și încredere pentru a... The post Psiholog appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

mix_items.title: Common NSAIDs in first trimester show no birth defect link, data suggest
mix_items.summary: Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), such as ibuprofen and naproxen, taken during the first trimester of pregnancy are not associated with an increased risk of major birth defects, according to a new study published in PLOS Medicine by Sharon Daniel of Ben-Gurion University of the Negev and Clalit Health Services, Beer-Sheva, Israel, and colleagues.

mix_items.title: „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase.
mix_items.summary: Într-o casă modestă din județul Vaslui, unde mirosul de lemne arse se amestecă cu zgomotul pașilor mici de copii și... Articolul „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase. apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță
mix_items.summary: În timp ce pentru mulți români Paștele este sărbătoarea revederii și a meselor în familie, pentru mulți vârstnici singuri aceste zile sunt printre cele mai grele din an. Singurătatea doare, lipsurile și sentimentul de abandon se simt mai apăsat tocmai atunci când toți ceilalți par... Post-ul De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

mix_items.title: Depressed mice successfully treated with smart contact lenses that zap their brains: New study
mix_items.summary: Scientists in South Korea have developed experimental contact lenses designed to send electrical signals through the retina and into brain regions linked to mood. In mice, the technology appeared to improve depression-like behavior.

mix_items.title: Summers are getting longer each year, and it isn't all fun and games
mix_items.summary: Do you have the sense that summers feel different than when you were younger? That they start earlier, arrive quickly and remain intense until the fall? If you live in the mid-latitudes of either the Northern or Southern Hemispheres, chances are you answered yes.

mix_items.title: New AI system classifies India rainfall better, cutting false alarms and missed heavy rain
mix_items.summary: AI can predict rainfall intensity better than several widely used forecasting models in tests using historical weather data from India. The new model reported in the International Journal of Mobile Communications shows that combining different forms of AI, along with advanced data-cleaning and optimization techniques, can make rainfall prediction more accurate and reliable, particularly when expressed in practical categories such as light, moderate, or heavy rain.

mix_items.title: France and Netherlands say all hantavirus contact cases negative
mix_items.summary: France's health minister said Thursday that 26 people in France identified as close contacts of hantavirus cases linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship had tested negative for the illness.

mix_items.title: The rules neurons follow to make sense of what we see
mix_items.summary: Even in the primary visual cortex, a brain region named for its specialized role in processing basic features of what the eyes see, not every neuron ends up answering the call to process properties of visual input. Maybe that's because each neuron receives a wide variety of inputs via thousands of circuit connections, or "synapses," and has to opt to respond to the visual information vs. something else.

mix_items.title: Steroid use falls, but creatine use climbs rapidly, study shows
mix_items.summary: U.S. teens report far less anabolic steroid use than they did two decades ago, but creatine use has risen rapidly in recent years, according to a new University of Michigan study. Combined with declining perceptions of steroid harm and slightly softer disapproval of steroid use, the findings suggest teens may be growing more accepting of muscle-enhancing products, including legal supplements and potentially steroids.

mix_items.title: NASA's Psyche spacecraft buzzing Mars on its way to a rare metal asteroid
mix_items.summary: A NASA spacecraft chasing a rare metal asteroid swings past Mars this week for a gravity boost, snapping thousands of pictures as practice for the main encounter in 2029.

mix_items.title: Experimental drug shrinks advanced kidney tumors, clears cancer in one patient
mix_items.summary: A new Phase I clinical trial provides proof of concept for a potential therapy for treatment-resistant cancers, particularly kidney cancer. In the new study, published in Cell Reports Medicine, researchers tested a novel kind of immunotherapy in individuals with different types of cancer.

mix_items.title: Combinations of climate extremes may prompt carbon budget rethink
mix_items.summary: Combined extreme climate events are likely to become more common in the future if carbon emissions continue to rise, a paper in Nature suggests. The study finds that the frequency of compound events—such as concurrent hot–wet and drought–heat extremes—is linked to cumulative carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions. In particular, the frequency of more severe events is predicted to escalate rapidly.

mix_items.title: Discovery of antimicrobial peptides in ant venom has far-reaching implications
mix_items.summary: In addition to serving as biochemical weapons for offense and defense, the venoms produced by ants in the subfamily Formicinae also fulfill additional roles. For example, the ants use it to protect their nests from pathogens. It has long been assumed that the primary constituent of these venoms, formic acid, was responsible for these functions.

mix_items.title: Large-scale eDNA survey reveals hidden factors that affect regional fish communities
mix_items.summary: As climate change and human activities continually ramp up, fish are forced to find ways to adapt. As fish move around to find more suitable habitats as ocean conditions shift, regional fish distributions change—which can have huge downstream effects on local ecosystems.

mix_items.title: Dinner at the door: Convenient healthy meals may ease depression symptoms
mix_items.summary: Making healthy meals more convenient through meal delivery services could improve depressive symptoms by removing some of the daily burdens that often accompany depression, according to a new University of Michigan study.

mix_items.title: Study finds one in eight adults carries hidden genetic risk—and reveals what it takes to act on it
mix_items.summary: When Mayo Clinic researchers sequenced the genomes of 484 seemingly healthy adults, they found that about 13% carried a serious, previously unrecognized genetic risk—conditions those patients did not know about and that standard care would likely miss.

mix_items.title: Mathematicians prove existence of Kaleidocycles then unlock their exact motion
mix_items.summary: Kaleidocycles are flexible polyhedral structures composed of rigid tetrahedra connected along their edges to form rotating rings. Each tetrahedron is a solid 3D polygon with four triangular faces (like a triangular pyramid), and the hinges connect neighboring units, enabling a smooth rotational motion of the ring without deforming the individual pieces. These mechanisms are often compared with the bubble rings blown by dolphins.

mix_items.title: NASA, Industry Prepare Cryogenic Fuel Technology Demo
mix_items.summary: NASA is collaborating with Eta Space of Rockledge, Florida, on an in‑orbit technology demonstration to advance a key capability for future deep space missions. The Liquid Oxygen Flight Demonstration, or LOXSAT, will test cryogenic fluid management technologies necessary for creating in-space propellant depots, essentially gas stations in space, that could support long-term exploration. During a […]

mix_items.title: Sleep and diet may matter more than exercise for buffering the health toll of chronic stress
mix_items.summary: When work gets stressful, the standard advice is familiar: exercise more, eat better, sleep more, and cut back on unhealthy habits. But our new research study suggests not all healthy habits offer the same protection from chronic work stress.

mix_items.title: Astrophysicists use 'space archaeology' to trace the history of a spiral galaxy
mix_items.summary: Billions of years ago, a young spiral galaxy began to grow in a crowded part of the universe. It pulled in gas and small companion galaxies, slowly building up the bright central region and sweeping spiral arms we see today.

mix_items.title: Will future missions to the moon be sustainable? It may depend on whom you ask
mix_items.summary: There's a new space race to the moon, and this time the ambitions are not just to visit but to stay. NASA's Artemis program aims to establish a long-term human presence on the lunar surface in the 2030s. China, India, Japan and a number of private companies all have lunar mission programs of their own.

mix_items.title: String theory is uniquely derived from basic assumptions about the universe, physicists show
mix_items.summary: If you could take an apple and break it into smaller and smaller parts, you would find molecules, then atoms, followed by subatomic particles like protons and the quarks and gluons that make them up. You might think you hit the bottom, but, according to string theorists, if you keep going to even smaller scales—about a billion billion times smaller than a proton—you will find more: tiny vibrating strings.

mix_items.title: How understanding art history can help doctors treat obesity
mix_items.summary: Understanding how obesity has been portrayed in art across the ages can make doctors more empathetic and less judgmental, which should lead to their patients achieving better outcomes, new research at the European Congress on Obesity (ECO 2026) argues.

mix_items.title: Sex-related differences in hoverfly eyes give insight into their aerodynamic powers
mix_items.summary: Many male hoverflies have bigger eyes than females, giving them the advantage of better optics and faster photoreceptors in high-speed pursuits to find a preferred partner to breed.

mix_items.title: A child's environment may shape how their brain solves problems
mix_items.summary: For decades, researchers have documented an achievement gap between children from higher- and lower-income families. On average, children with more resources perform better in school and on cognitive tests.

mix_items.title: The liver's immune cells might be the key to curing hepatitis B
mix_items.summary: Fifteen years ago, doctors in Europe noticed a remarkable thing happening in people with chronic hepatitis B infections. When patients went off their medications, the virus started to come back—and then some of the patients were cured. Something was goading their immune systems to go after the virus as the infection rebounded.

mix_items.title: Study finds more than 84% of dogs show signs of fear, anxiety
mix_items.summary: A dog trembling during a thunderstorm or backing away from a stranger may seem like an isolated reaction—yet new research suggests these moments are far from rare. In fact, the majority of dogs may experience some level of fear or anxiety more often than their owners, according to a new study that analyzed behavior data from tens of thousands of pets across the United States.

mix_items.title: This simple amino acid supplement greatly reduces Alzheimer’s damage
mix_items.summary: A new study suggests a surprisingly simple compound could help fight Alzheimer’s disease. Researchers found that arginine—an inexpensive amino acid already considered safe—can reduce the buildup of toxic amyloid proteins in the brain, a hallmark of the disease. In animal models, oral arginine not only lowered harmful protein deposits but also improved behavior and reduced brain inflammation.

mix_items.title: This 2-pound dinosaur is rewriting what scientists know about evolution
mix_items.summary: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dinosaurs. The fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis reveals that these animals became tiny before developing their later specialized features, such as stubby arms and ant-eating adaptations. Weighing under two pounds, the dinosaur is one of the smallest known from South America.

mix_items.title: Scientists finally solve the mystery of yeast’s tiny centromeres
mix_items.summary: Scientists have uncovered how brewer’s yeast developed its unusually tiny centromeres, the DNA regions that guide chromosome separation during cell division. By studying related yeast species, researchers found centromeres that appear to represent evolutionary halfway points. These structures seem to have formed from retrotransposons—mobile “jumping genes” in the genome. The discovery shows how DNA once considered genomic junk can be transformed into essential chromosome machinery.

mix_items.title: Light-guided evolution creates proteins that can switch, sense, and compute
mix_items.summary: Researchers have created a method called optovolution that uses light to guide the evolution of proteins with dynamic behaviors. By engineering yeast cells so their survival depended on proteins switching states at the right time, scientists could rapidly select the best-performing variants. The technique produced new light-sensitive proteins that respond to different colors and improved optogenetic systems. It even evolved a protein that behaves like a tiny logic gate, activating genes only when two signals are present.

mix_items.title: Scientists discover hidden species among Borneo’s “fanged frogs”
mix_items.summary: DNA is revealing that many animals once thought to be a single species may actually be several hidden ones. But research on Bornean fanged frogs shows the line between species can be blurry—an important challenge when deciding what wildlife needs protection most.

mix_items.title: Lake Coatepeque
mix_items.summary: Set amid El Salvador’s modern, active volcanic landscape, tranquil blue waters fill a caldera formed by ancient eruptions. The post Lake Coatepeque appeared first on NASA Science .

mix_items.title: Scientists stunned to find signs of ancient life in a place no one expected
mix_items.summary: While exploring ancient seabeds in Morocco, scientists discovered strange wrinkle-like textures in deep-water sediments that shouldn’t have been there. These structures are usually made by sunlight-loving microbial mats in shallow waters. But the rocks formed far below the reach of light, suggesting a different explanation. Evidence points to chemosynthetic microbes—organisms powered by chemical reactions—creating the mats in the dark depths of an ancient ocean.

mix_items.title: Scientists finally reveal why mint feels cold
mix_items.summary: Scientists have revealed how the body’s microscopic cold sensor, TRPM8, detects both chilly temperatures and the cooling effect of menthol. The discovery finally shows how the sensation of “cool” works at the molecular level—and could inspire new treatments for pain and eye disorders.

mix_items.title: Bird droppings helped build one of ancient Peru’s most powerful kingdoms
mix_items.summary: New research suggests seabird guano helped transform the Chincha Kingdom into one of the most prosperous societies in ancient Peru. Chemical clues in centuries-old maize show farmers fertilized their crops with guano gathered from nearby islands, dramatically boosting yields in the desert landscape. The resulting agricultural surplus fueled trade, population growth, and regional influence.

joke_ro.text: M-am dus in bucatarie dupa apa. M-am intors cu sendvis si pace interioara.

satire_ro.title: Terasă din Vitan, declarată rezervaţie naturală după ce un client a găsit în ciorbă o specie rară de muscă
